english: 626. ཉེར་ (nyer) near, though Tibetan dictionaries write ཉེ་བར་ (nye bar)) to make use of one's intellectual powers. To do this rightly forms part of Buddhist wisdom (v. Köpp. I, 436) and instruction (Dzl. མཛངས་ (mdzangs), 7, where Sch.'s version is incorr.), being divided into four divisions or degrees (Burn.); saṅs-rgyás-la dkón-pai _du-šés nyé-bar bžág-pa (sangs rgyas la dkon pai 'du shes nye bar bzhag pa) to apply to Buddha the notion of rareness Tar. 5, 13. — 4. intensely, urgently, speedily, jigs-pa nyé-bar ži ('jigs pa nye bar zhi) fear is speedily allayed Glr.; nad nyé-bar _tso (nad nye bar 'tsho) the disease is speedily cured Thgy.; nyé-bar lén-pa (nye bar len pa) Mil., Thgy. to seize eagerly, to strive for earnestly, to aspire to, esp. to the re-birth as a human being; cf. also nyer-lén (nyer len); nyé-bar mkó-ba (nye bar mkho ba) of urgent necessity, frq. Tar. nyer _pel (nyer 'phel) it increases rapidly Med. IV. sbst. v. nyé-šiṅ (nye shing). Comp. nye-skór (nye skor) Sch. nye-_kór (nye 'khor) those about us, the company around us, khyed-ráṅ-gi nye-_kór-gyi ldóm-bu-ba (khyed rang gi nye 'khor gyi ldom bu ba) a beggar belonging to the people around you Mil.; esp. relations, kindred, des nye-_kór yaṅ jigs-kyis yoṅ (des nye 'khor yang 'jigs kyis yong) in this way family-connections are formed of themselves Mil. — nye-_mkón (nye 'khon) = nye-ríṅ (nye ring) Cs. (?) — nye-grógs (nye grogs) neighbour, fellow-creature Cs. — nye-čár (nye char) now Sch. — nyé-dag (nye dag) Cs., nyé-du (nye du), and most frq. nye-_brél (nye 'brel) (nyen-_brél (nyen 'brel)) kindred, relations (these being considered a main obstacle to moral perfection, they are to be shunned accordingly). — nye-gnás (nye gnas) disciple, khyéd-kyi nye-gnás bgyio (khyed kyi nye gnas bgyio), nye-gnás-su mčhio (nye gnas su mchio) I wish to become your disciple Dzl. — nye-tsán (nye tshan), nye-rígs (nye rigs) relative, kinsman. — nye-ríṅ (nye ring) 1. near and far, near and distant relations. 2. distance, sgor nye-ríṅ či-tsam yod (sgor nye ring ci tsam yod) how far is it from here to the gate? 3. partial, rgyál-po nye-ríṅ čes (rgyal po nye ring ces) the king is very partial Glr., nye-ríṅ-méd-pa (nye ring med pa) impartial Glr. — nye-lám (nye lam) near; now Sch.
damage, mishap, accident (syn. to bar-čad (bar chad)), nye-žo-méd-par (nye zho med par) without an accident, safely Dzl.
or nyé-bai šiṅ (nye bai shing) Med., a tree the fruits of which are used as a sweet medicine.

1. = nye (nye), ngen-kór (ngen 'khor), or nyen-skór (nyen skor) = nye-_kór (nye 'khor) a relative, Pth.: nyen-_kór žig yin (nyen 'khor zhig yin) he is a kinsman; also alone, like gnyen (gnyen). — 2. with a vb.: danger, risk, myúr-du jig-nyen yod (myur du 'jig nyen yod) there is a danger of its being soon destroyed Glr.; dmyál-bar gro-nyén yda (dmyal bar gro nyen yod) there is a danger of going to hell; sróg-gi bar-čád-du gró-bai nyen yod (srog gi bar chad du gro bai nyen yod) Mil. of risking one's life; *dúṅ-nyen* (*dung nyen*) C. he has the chance of receiving a good beating; occasionally also: to be near, to impend, in reference to happy events; in col. language it is simply used for danger, nyén-čan (nyen can) dangerous, e.g. lam (lam), las (las), sbrul (sbrul) etc.
pf. nyén-to (nyen to), to be pained, pinched, pressed hard, e.g. by hunger, cold, enemies; to toil and moil, to labour hard, to drudge; v. baṅ (bang).
1. Sch. to tan, curry, dress, make soft. — 2. W., also *nyer-kád táṅ-če* (*nyer kad tang ce*), to snarl, growl. — 3. W. to tarry, stay, linger (snyér-ba (snyer ba) for bsnár-ba? (bsnar ba?)).
W. for ryér-ma (ryer ma), red pepper.
or nyé-bar lén-pa (nye bar len pa), is said to be = rgyui rgyu (rgyui rgyu), original cause.

I. sbst. any thing wrong or noxious, or liable to become so, and the consequences of it; hence 1. evil, calamity, damage, nyés-pa tams-čád deï lús-la _duo (nyes pa thams cad de'i lus la 'duo) all sorts of plagues are collecting upon his body Dzl.; lo-nyés (lo nyes) a bad harvest, failure of crops, lo-nyés byúṅ-bai tse (lo nyes byung bai tshe) when
